_publications._publicationTitle: C-Fiber Loss as a Possible Cause of Neuropathic Pain in Schwannomatosis.

_contextSnippet: Regarding LEP amplitudes, no differences were detected in the patient group compared to matched controls (N2: F(1,32) = 0.224, p = 0.640; P2: F(1,31) = 0.110, p = 0.742; N2P2: F(1,31) = 0.007, p = 0.933). Latencies tended to be faster in the patients group
details: LEP measurements showed no differences in amplitudes compared to controls (N2: F(1,32) = 0.224, p = 0.640; P2: F(1,31) = 0.110, p = 0.742). Latencies tended to be faster in patients but did not reach significance. 10 of 18 patients showed N2 latencies below 95% confidence interval of controls.
